finding_results.notes: Roughened all four cited beats. Nate's line at 261 now has a false start and concrete reference to Mallick's repeated visits instead of the abstract caption 'accuracy.' Paul's line at 285 now opens with a self-correction ('I'm not—nobody's calling him a monster') and anchors to a specific day ('She knew it Saturday') per his stress-tell of citing times. Paul's line at 293 now breaks mid-sentence with an exhale and restarts with a trailing qualification ('Not everything. Just why') instead of the polished metaphor 'laundering.' Doug's line at 345 now states the observation concretely ('None of us have told her the real one yet') and walks it back with 'Just saying,' matching his retraction habit. Additionally restored Cliff's self-correction at 219 ('who made a—I'm saying Joel doesn't own her') from the snapshot, where the current chapter had cleaned it into a finished sentence.

summary: Single finding addressed across four late-deck dialogue beats plus one snapshot-texture restoration. Nate, Paul, and Doug still land the same decisions (Nate drops the bit, Paul commits to telling Mallick, Doug punctures the shared lie) but each beat now arrives through character-specific friction: false starts, self-corrections, mid-sentence breaks, concrete scene references, and trailing retractions rather than standalone thesis lines. Cliff's self-correction in the debate section was also restored from the pre-revision snapshot where it had been cleaned into a finished clause. No structural, continuity, or heading-contract changes.
